reasoning: SPY at 680.95, testing the 5-day Carry support at 680.4 from above. Ribbon is BEAR (fast 682.7 < pivot 683.2 < slow 684.5), all sectors down, risk-off rotation active. The Carry support at 680.4 (2★) is the critical level — if it holds, a rebound toward 682-683 reference resistance is possible; if it breaks, the next floor is 675.95, creating significant downside exposure. VIX falling (+opportunity for support to firm), but environment remains bearish.

primary.condition: if SPY holds 680.4 Carry support on first test
primary.notes: VIX falling is an early signal that support may firm. Rebound attempt toward first reference resistance at 682 / pivot EMA at 683.2.

secondary.condition: if SPY breaks below 680.4 Carry support
secondary.notes: Risk-off rotation + BEAR ribbon increases odds of flush through support. No meaningful defense until pivot S1 at 675.95. Sector weakness may accelerate break.

tertiary.condition: if 680.4 is tested multiple times without break
tertiary.notes: If support proves sticky across multiple touches, price may consolidate between 680.4 and 682-683, waiting for volatility catalyst.

key_observations: 680.4 Carry support is the pivotal level — price is already within $0.55, first decision point comes immediately at market open.
Ribbon stack is inverted (BEAR) and spread is 180 cents — high conviction downside bias is encoded in the indicator structure.
All four reference supports (680, 679, 678, 677) are thin (1★ each) and will not hold a break — if 680.4 fails, next floor is 675.95 per-chance levels.
Resistance is distant: 697.08 is 16 points away; even 682-683 reference levels are 1-2 points up. Asymmetry favors downside continuation.
VIX falling (-2.4%) is an early sign of potential volatility compression, but sector rotation (XLK -2.86%, XLF -2.29%, XLE -1.75%) confirms institutional risk-off, not tactical oversold.
